output:
I’m reviewing story `21-5-delete-workflow-machine-and-hierarchical-flow` as a code review task. I’ll inspect the branch diff and the relevant tests first, then check for correctness, security, architecture fit, and whether the implementation appears to cover the intended acceptance criteria.The implementation is uncommitted on `master`, so I’m reviewing the working tree diff directly. Next I’m pulling the story artifact and the key file diffs to line up the intended scope against the actual deletions and rewiring.I’ve got the scope diff. The risky area is not the import rewrites, it’s the refactor that split parser and machine logic into new modules while deleting the old shim. I’m reading the new `workflow-execution`, `workflow-machines`, and `workflow-runner` implementations now to check for behavior drift behind the updated tests.I found something odd: `git diff` shows `workflow-machine.ts` as a tracked deletion, but `git show HEAD:...` says the path is absent in `HEAD`. I’m checking the index/tree state directly before drawing conclusions, because that usually means the worktree is based on a conflicted or partially-staged state.I have enough context to start forming findings, but I want one pass of targeted verification against the refactored modules so I’m not relying only on static inspection. I’m running the hierarchical parser tests and the workflow-engine tests that historically cover the deleted shim’s behavior.The refactor itself is mostly coherent. I’m checking one more angle before I write the verdict: whether the story’s verification/AC side was actually completed, not just whether the new modules compile in isolation.The targeted checks are clean so far, including `npm run build` and the boundary test slice. I’m doing one final broad check with the full Vitest suite because this story’s main risk is regression from moving exports and flow logic around.The code path checks are clean. The only remaining concern is acceptance-coverage hygiene: the implementation appears to satisfy the technical ACs, but I’m verifying whether the required proof artifact and verification trail were actually produced.<verdict>fail</verdict>

<issues>
1. AC14 is not currently satisfiable as implemented. Running `npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | rg 'workflow-machine|hierarchical-flow'` still produces output because [workflow-machine.test.ts:182](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts#L182), [workflow-machine.test.ts:183](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts#L183), and [workflow-machine.test.ts:184](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-machine.test.ts#L184) trigger `TS2352`. The errors are in a file whose path still contains `workflow-machine`, so the story’s prescribed verification command does not come back empty.

2. The required proof artifact is missing: [verification/21-5-delete-workflow-machine-and-hierarchical-flow-proof.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/verification/21-5-delete-workflow-machine-and-hierarchical-flow-proof.md). That leaves AC/verification coverage incomplete even though the code changes themselves look sound.
</issues>

I did not find a runtime correctness, security, or architecture defect in the refactor itself. The core implementation checks I ran were clean: `npm run build` passed, `npx vitest run` passed with 4977/4977 tests, `npx vitest run -t 'boundar'` passed, the deleted files are gone, and there are no remaining `from ...workflow-machine` / `from ...hierarchical-flow` imports in `src/`.

The failure is on acceptance coverage, not on the main code path.
